topic: Stabilization & quality hardening for Fabric (MCP-first multi-client tool)

findings.finding: Claude Code does NOT support AGENTS.md natively (uses CLAUDE.md exclusively); Codex CLI + Cursor do.
findings.detail: GitHub issue #6235 open with no Anthropic timeline. Symlink CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md is the canonical workaround.

findings.finding: MCP server timeouts are #1 production stability issue (24% of failures); TS MCP clients enforce hard 60s limit not extended by progress notifications.

findings.detail: Drift between declared input schema and implementation is recurring.

findings.finding: Cursor has ~40 active-tool ceiling across all MCP servers; exceeding silently degrades.
findings.detail: Each tool definition burns context tokens. Keep Fabric tool surface ≤5.

findings.finding: Codex CLI uses TOML at ~/.codex/config.toml ([mcp_servers.<name>]); NOT JSON.
findings.detail: Cursor + Claude both accept identical mcpServers JSON; Codex is the format outlier.

findings.finding: MCP Tool Annotations (2025-03-26 spec): readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint are official.
findings.detail: Clients use these for auto-approval / confirmation UX. Fabric's read-only tools should declare them.

findings.finding: Knip has displaced ts-prune as the dead-code standard.
findings.detail: Detects unused exports, files, deps, duplicate exports, unused class/enum members.

findings.finding: Claude Code session lifecycle leaks server processes (issue #15945, 70+ zombies).
findings.detail: Servers must install SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers and exit cleanly.

findings.finding: fsyncgate: on many filesystems an fsync error marks pages clean and the application has no way to retry.
findings.detail: Treat ledger as poisoned on fsync error; rotate file; surface via doctor.

best_practices.practice: Use Zod for tool input validation (SDK requires it as peer dep)
best_practices.rationale: Validation runs before handler; invalid input returns structured error without executing.

best_practices.practice: Implement initialize → response → initialized handshake exactly; never batch initialize
best_practices.rationale: Capability negotiation is mandatory; some clients reject batched initialize.

best_practices.practice: Atomic file writes via atomically (modern, TS-native) or write-file-atomic (battle-tested)
best_practices.rationale: Both write tmp file in same dir then rename(2) — atomic on POSIX. atomically queues writes to same path.

best_practices.practice: JSONL append-only ledger: pair appends with explicit fsync, periodic snapshots, tolerate trailing partial line on read
best_practices.rationale: fsync required for durability; fsyncgate makes errors unrecoverable.

best_practices.practice: Enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes in tsconfig
best_practices.rationale: Two highest-impact strict flags not in strict:true; catch real array/record bugs.
Type-aware rules catch floating promises, misused promises, unnecessary conditions.

best_practices.practice: Declare MCP tool annotations explicitly (readOnlyHint, idempotentHint)
best_practices.rationale: Improves UX in clients that auto-approve safe tools.

best_practices.practice: Snapshot test config emission per client (Claude/Codex/Cursor) with vitest .snap
best_practices.rationale: CI fails on drift; -u to update intentionally.
Prevents the #1 MCP failure category (38% schema mismatch).

alternatives.pros: TS-native, faster (~10x with fsyncWait), queues same-path writes, zero deps
Knip detects exports + deps + duplicates; 80+ tool plugins
alternatives.verdict: Knip recommended; ts-prune in maintenance mode

alternatives.pros: Reveals tests that 'cover' but don't assert
alternatives.verdict: Targeted use on rule-distribution core only

alternatives.pros: One file on disk, two filenames, zero drift
alternatives.cons: Only safe if Claude content is identical to AGENTS
alternatives.verdict: Recommended where Fabric content is identical

alternatives.cons: Carries dead code, complicates test matrix
alternatives.verdict: Delete in major bump after one minor with deprecation warning
Split emitters by FORMAT (JSON vs TOML), not by client

pitfalls.mitigation: Keep Fabric tool surface ≤5; document constraint

pitfalls.issue: Hard 60s timeout in TS MCP clients (not extended by progress)
pitfalls.mitigation: Keep tool calls <10s; chunk large rule sets
Install SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers; clean exit deadline

pitfalls.issue: fsync errors are unrecoverable on many filesystems
pitfalls.mitigation: Rotate ledger on fsync error; report via doctor
Never include initialize in a batch request

pitfalls.issue: Schema drift without contract tests (38% MCP failures)
pitfalls.mitigation: Golden-file fixtures asserting SDK-emitted JSON Schema matches committed copy
On read, tolerate trailing partial line; doctor truncates
Write one canonical file + symlink, or snapshot-test equality

pitfalls.issue: Cross-client config format divergence (JSON vs TOML)
pitfalls.mitigation: Split emitters by format, not by client

codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Add both; expect 1-2 days fixup; major bug-class reduction

codebase_gaps.gap: No explicit MCP tool annotations on packages/server/src/tools/*
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Add annotations matching actual behavior (all 3 tools appear read-only)

codebase_gaps.gap: No Knip configuration for dead-code identification
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: pnpm dlx knip baseline now; rerun after each client removal

codebase_gaps.gap: Atomic write usage inconsistent — events.jsonl uses raw appendFile while snapshots use atomicWriteText
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Standardize on atomically; explicit fsync + ledger rotation on error

codebase_gaps.gap: No snapshot tests for emitted client configs
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Vitest .snap per (client × scope) — 6 snapshots for 3 clients × {project, user}

codebase_gaps.gap: No contract tests asserting MCP tool JSON Schema against committed fixture
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Golden-file fixture per tool; CI fails on drift

codebase_gaps.gap: No MCP handshake conformance test (initialize → initialized → tools/list → tools/call)
fabric doctor doesn't detect JSONL ledger tail corruption
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: doctor reads ledger, tolerates trailing partial line, reports; --fix rewrites
SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers, transport close, deadline exit

sources.title: MCP Specification 2025-11-25
sources.key_takeaway: Authoritative spec for lifecycle, capabilities, error codes

sources.title: AGENTS.md cross-tool guide
sources.key_takeaway: Claude Code uses CLAUDE.md only; symlink workaround
